claim: RNN Encoder-Decoder maps a source symbol sequence into a representation and decodes it into a target sequence, trained to maximize target probability given source.
Attention in early NMT was introduced to avoid the fixed-vector bottleneck by allowing a model to soft-search relevant source parts while predicting a target word.
GNMT used deep LSTM encoder/decoder layers with attention and residual connections, plus low-precision inference and wordpieces for rare-word handling.
GNMT reported an average 60% reduction in translation errors versus Google's phrase-based production system on isolated simple sentences.
Subword units were proposed for NMT rare-word handling, with Sennrich et al. publishing the ACL 2016 paper on BPE-style subword translation.
SentencePiece can train subword models directly from raw sentences rather than requiring pre-tokenized word sequences.
Hugging Face documents tokenization as normalization, pre-tokenization, model, post-processing, and decoding.
OpenAI tiktoken describes BPE tokenization as reversible and lossless for converting tokens back into original text.
Semi-autoregressive NMT keeps global autoregressive structure while producing multiple successive words in parallel at each time step.
Speculative decoding drafts several tokens with an approximation model and verifies them with the larger model, accelerating inference without changing the output distribution.
Multi-token prediction trains LLMs to predict several future tokens at once using multiple output heads over a shared trunk.
Reflexion uses verbal feedback and episodic memory to improve later agent attempts without updating weights.
Self-Refine uses iterative feedback and refinement from the same LLM without requiring supervised training data or RL.
Boot.dev markets hands-on coding, game-like curriculum, portfolio projects, Backend/DevOps paths, and an AI mentor named Boots.
Boot.dev Training Grounds advertises personalized challenges, spaced repetition, three challenge types, custom prompts, XP, streaks, quests, and more than 40,000 student-rated challenges.
Anthropic's Artifacts put generated code, documents, or website designs in a dedicated real-time workspace beside the chat.
Anthropic tool-use docs describe a pattern where the model returns structured tool calls and the application or server executes them.
Claude Code is documented as an agentic coding tool that reads codebases, edits files, runs commands, and integrates with development tools.
Claude for Chrome is a beta browser extension that can navigate, click, fill forms, and work with Claude Code and Cowork; Anthropic flags browser AI prompt-injection and sensitive-action risks.
